## [00:00 - 03:06]

Hey, this is Deepesh and this video is focused on the post purchase experience. So this screen that you can see here is actually an ad set which has been targeted to people after they've actually made a purchase. So this is if you go back to the full funnel system, you have cold, warm and hot. This is existing customers, the fourth part of the funnel. Actually the biggest part I see people meet leaving money on the table. There's different ways to monetize customers once they've made their first purchase. And people seem to neglect Facebook ads. There's no reason why you can't serve them ads once they've made a purchase. Emails are possibly the most obvious way that people end up, store owners end up generating more revenue from their customers. Messenger bots are another way as well if you can get them into a sequence and keep them up to date with latest releases or anything like that. But actually I found that once you start to understand your product better and get an idea for when and why they would repurchase, then you can really now that post purchase targeting and actually squeeze more value out of them as well. So this is an example of an e-com store where we're actually selling candles. And through research we found that most people burn through their candles within roughly 30 to 60 days. So what we figured out was what happened then if we retargeted people 30 days after the last purchase and up to 180 days. The reason for 180 was because we were using website conversion, sorry website customer audiences, you can go beyond that if you just load up your email audience and you just push everyone that's purchased between 30 days and one whatever period ago. So with this particular ad set and I'll just go in to show you how simple it was to set up, I've created two audiences. So people that purchased in the last 180 days, website customer audience and I've excluded people that purchased in the last 30 days. So it's a subset audience there and no particular targeting beyond that. We're only selling in the States, no age, gender, no interest, nothing else. For this one I did edit the placements but I would generally recommend just going with all placements and doing some analysis afterwards. And then the types of ads that I had set up for this one was simply new arrivals. So the idea was if you've been using candles, you've had a great experience and you want to buy again, then we'll just push our latest arrivals and fortunately for us we had a big 40% discount we could give existing customers as well. So when you combine those together you can see the crazy, crazy numbers we got back where we spent $1,500 and generated $40,000 in return, 2,500% return on investment, $2 cost for acquisitions

and you just see the whole thing just kind of blew up. Now we've tested multiple ads, some of them were limited up time offers so you can see some crazy results here. ## [06:12 - 09:12]

offer upsells and cross sells and this is another thing that I've worked really well in the past before. So to give you an example if for example you're selling jackets you could maybe 14 days after their first purchase maybe even seven days even straight after the first purchase upsell them into something complimentary, some trousers or a shirt or something like that and often you'll find that because the purchase from you already if they've had a great experience and they maybe were just thinking of buying something else you can nudge them into another sale as well. But Facebook ads themselves I think and having gone through so many different ad accounts when it comes to e-commerce the post purchase is the bit where people are leaving money on the table. So experiment here see what works best for your brand. There are for example you could run a thank you ad this has worked for many many stores literally after they purchase you might do it day after just give them a bit of kind of respite after the purchase and then maybe a day to three days after just serve up that thank you ad is a daily unique ad sorry daily unique reach so they only see they add once a day and if you really want to kind of smooth the process out run that for one to seven days after the first purchase and if they've watched more than 25% then don't serve them the same video again. So just make sure that you don't keep pushing the thank you message over and over again and then maybe follow up in the in the sequence after that so roughly after their products arrived let's say your delivery times are three to five days give it maybe seven eight days after at the moment they've received their products is the moment that they've reached peak excitement so there's been some studies done on purchasing and kind of how that has an impact on the human mind buying a product gives you a high level of satisfaction receiving the product in your hand boosts it tenfold and that's why you'll see at events and seminars and things like that often when you sign up for a course or coaching program and you fill out a piece of paper they'll give you something physical they'll give you a t-shirt or a starter pack or something because actually buying something online doesn't have the same satisfaction it has when as opposed to actually receiving something in the hand and we can use that to a advantage post purchase if we know that the customers now receive the product and you have to be really careful about this then start to serve ads on the next logical part of their purchase journey whether it's buying more of the same products or brand related products that's the time to capture them as well and for people that are running kind of cross cells and upsell promotions post purchase that's probably for me quite contentious

area so Amazon for example on the product page will show people have also viewed this or people that bought this bought that etc etc it works for some brands but not not all and this is something I
